working: Email open rates are exceptional and far above the 25-35% health/nonprofit benchmark: 90-day average open is 52.15%, with the May Alumni eNews hitting 55.71% and the June Alumni Final Reminder 45.86%.

Newsletters reliably lift site traffic: Staff-Client eNews - May 2026 (5/19) drove the biggest lift at +66.5% (28.3 vs 17.0 baseline daily sessions), and the most recent send, Alumni eNews: Final Reminder - June 2026 (6/3), lifted +65.2% (34.7 vs 21.0).
SEO is gaining momentum: GSC clicks jumped from 6 to 26 vs the 6/17 run, impressions more than doubled (1,626 to 3,684), and impression-weighted average position improved from 17.12 to 15.71.
Organic Search grew to 816 sessions (38.4%) from 810, and Organic Social ticked up to 36 sessions (1.7%) from 32, while total window sessions held steady at 2,126 vs 2,122.
not_working: UTM leakage is the single biggest gap: every one of the 9 in-window sends shows 0 email_sessions_3d but 21-53 direct_sessions_3d. The June Final Reminder shows 0 email / 49 direct, and the +66.5% Staff-Client send shows 0 email / 53 direct, so GA4 is mis-crediting newsletter traffic as Direct.

Click engagement is weak despite great opens: 90-day average click rate is just 2.07%, individual sends range 0.40%-1.79%, and click-to-open sits at 0.88%-3.77% - readers open but rarely click through.

Several sends had negative or near-zero lift: Alumni eNews - May 2026 (5/2) at -50.2%, Staff-Client eNews - April 2026 at -15.7%, Alumni eNews - June 2026 (5/27) at -9.8%, and Reminder 2: Alumni eNews - May at -1.3%.
Organic Social remains a minor channel at 1.7% (36 sessions), below a 5% target, and Paid Search is effectively dormant at 1 session.
Tracked keywords returning GSC data dropped from 22 to 12 vs the 6/17 run, worth checking even as overall impressions rose.
do_next: Add utm_source=constantcontact&utm_medium=email (plus utm_campaign per send) to all newsletter links in Constant Contact so GA4 credits Email instead of Direct - this is the highest-value fix and will make every future send measurable.

Repromote the content/cadence behind the two strongest sends: Staff-Client eNews - May (+66.5%, 5/19) and Alumni Final Reminder - June (+65.2%, 6/3) both landed early/mid-week and outperformed - model future sends on their timing and topics.
Lift click-through by tightening CTAs: opens average 52% but click-to-open is only ~1.5-2%, so a single clear primary CTA and link near the top should convert more of those strong opens.
Keep feeding the pages that are gaining SEO traction (impressions up 2.3x, position improving to 15.71) and investigate why tracked-keyword coverage fell from 22 to 12.
summary: All three sources (GA4, Constant Contact, GSC) are live. Traffic held steady at 2,126 sessions over 90 days with Direct leading at 53%, but missing UTM tags on every newsletter are masking real email impact as Direct; SEO is the clear bright spot with clicks up 6 to 26 and position improving.
